---
title: "Corpus"
subtitle: "28 documents, 1838–1865"
---

The corpus spans Lincoln's public career from the Lyceum Address (1838) to the Last Public Address (1865), drawn from the University of Michigan *Collected Works of Abraham Lincoln* digital edition.

Register, authorship confidence, and analytical priority are the three primary corpus-control variables. All frequency claims in the analysis must be register-controlled before publication. See [Corpus Register](skills/corpus_register.md) for register definitions and [Annotation Protocol](skills/annotation_protocol.md) for the confidence scoring procedure.

Source texts (Stage 2 markdown) are rendered at `corpus/text/{id}.html` and are linked in the table below.

```{ojs}
manifest = await FileAttachment("corpus/corpus_manifest.json").json()
docs = manifest.documents
```

## Filter

```{ojs}
viewof reg = Inputs.select(
  ["All", ...manifest.register_enum],
  {label: "Register", value: "All"}
)

viewof pri = Inputs.select(
  ["All", "critical", "high", "medium"],
  {label: "Priority", value: "All"}
)
```

## Documents

```{ojs}
filtered = docs.filter(d =>
  (reg === "All" || d.register === reg) &&
  (pri === "All" || d.analytical_priority === pri)
)

Inputs.table(
  filtered.map(d => ({
    id: d.id,
    title: d.short_title,
    date: d.date,
    register: d.register,
    authorship: d.authorship,
    conf: d.authorship_confidence,
    priority: d.analytical_priority,
    stage: d.pipeline_stage_completed,
    risk: d.risk_flags.length > 0 ? d.risk_flags.join(", ") : "—"
  })),
  {
    columns: ["id", "title", "date", "register", "authorship", "conf", "priority", "stage", "risk"],
    header: {
      id: "ID",
      title: "Document",
      date: "Date",
      register: "Register",
      authorship: "Authorship",
      conf: "Auth. Conf.",
      priority: "Priority",
      stage: "Stage",
      risk: "Risk Flags"
    },
    format: {
      conf: x => x.toFixed(2),
      id: id => htl.html`<a href="corpus/text/${id}.html">${id}</a>`
    },
    rows: 30
  }
)
```

## Register Distribution

```{ojs}
reg_counts = d3.rollup(docs, v => v.length, d => d.register)
reg_rows = Array.from(reg_counts, ([register, count]) => ({register, count}))
  .sort((a, b) => b.count - a.count)

Inputs.table(reg_rows, {
  columns: ["register", "count"],
  header: {register: "Register", count: "Documents"}
})
```

## Authorship Notes

All documents are authored by Lincoln (`lincoln_sole`) or primarily by Lincoln (`lincoln_primary`). Documents with `authorship_confidence < 0.90` have transcription variants or co-authorship complications; all frequency analyses must be run twice — once for the full corpus and once for the `authorship_confidence ≥ 0.95` subset.

The Lincoln-Douglas debate transcriptions (doc_006a–g, confidence 0.82–0.83) have the lowest confidence in the corpus due to newspaper transcription noise. They are annotated with a −0.05 confidence adjustment applied to all Lincoln turns. See [Annotation Protocol](skills/annotation_protocol.md) for the full special-document protocol.

The First Inaugural (doc_009, confidence 0.93) contains Seward-drafted passages; statistical runs for that document must be computed twice: full document and Lincoln-sole sentences only.
